idea: 1p2c warp-spec baseline: TM=128 WARP_N=128 stages=3 — starting point
Reduce TILE_M 128->64, switch 1p2c->1p1c: 512 CTAs vs 256, halves SMEM from 163KB to 104KB, 4.5 waves vs 2.25
Persistent 114 CTA STAGES=4 WARP_N=128: SMEM=128KB fills carveout, only 1 CTA/SM, drops occupancy 24%->7.8%, regression -27T
Persistent WARP_N=96 STAGES=3: 2048 not divisible by 96 (22 tiles, last partial), severe boundary overhead, 148T <<< 228T. N=128 required for 2048.
Persistent STAGES=2 WARP_N=128: SMEM=80KB, but 2-stage pipeline insufficient to hide K=16384 memory latency; compute utilization extremely low 139T. Persistent approach abandoned.
1p2c ping-pong TILE_N=256: 2 consumers split N-dim, 256 CTAs. SMEM=188KB too large, reduced occupancy. Reverted.
Phase 2 cu fine-tune: unroll 4 + launch_bounds(256,1). Regression -48T. launch_bounds(256,1) may force register spilling.
TILE_K=128 STAGES=3: CUDA failure invalid argument at runtime. TMA box shape 128 may exceed limits or swizzle mismatch.
WARP_N=256: 211T vs 228T. CTA count dropped to 256 (2.2 waves), wave quantization hurts more than larger N helps.
STAGES=2: 199T vs 228T. 2 stages insufficient to hide K=16384 memory latency, same conclusion as iter005.
